title: Croatian in 60 Days — the Pareto Sprint

days.title: The Croatian alphabet
days.objective: Learn all 30 letters so you can read ANY word aloud correctly.
days.paretoFocus: Croatian is perfectly phonetic. Master the alphabet once — especially č/ć/š/ž/dž/đ/lj/nj — and pronunciation is solved forever.
drills: Listen to and repeat all 30 letters.
Read 15 words aloud from the cheatsheet.
Drill the 8 special letters until automatic.

items: Recall the sounds of č, š, ž, lj, nj.

days.title: Greetings & politeness
days.objective: Open and close interactions politely.
days.paretoFocus: A few fixed phrases (bok, dobar dan, hvala, molim) cover almost every first interaction.
drills: Learn bok / dobar dan / dobro jutro / doviđenja.
Practise hvala / molim / oprostite / da / ne.
Role-play greeting someone formally and casually.

days.title: Introduce yourself
days.objective: Say your name and nationality and ask the same.
days.paretoFocus: 'Ja sam… / Kako se zoveš?' opens any conversation. Maximum reuse for minimal effort.
drills: Build your intro: Ja sam… (name, nationality).
Learn Kako se zoveš? / Odakle si?

items: Give your full intro from memory.

days.title: Three genders
days.objective: Recognise masculine, feminine and neuter nouns by their endings.
days.paretoFocus: Unlike many languages, Croatian gender is usually visible in the ending — a huge shortcut you should exploit from day one.
drills: Note: there are NO articles in Croatian.

items: Guess the gender of 8 new nouns.
Why is there no 'the' in Croatian?

days.title: Personal pronouns
days.objective: Know ja, ti, on/ona/ono, mi, vi, oni/one.
days.paretoFocus: Pronouns frame every verb you'll learn — and Croatian often drops them because verb endings already show the subject.
drills: Understand why pronouns are often omitted.

items: Choose ti/vi for: a friend, a stranger, a group.

days.title: biti (to be) — present
days.objective: Use the short and negative forms of 'to be'.
days.paretoFocus: biti is the most common verb and the helper for the past tense later. The short forms (sam, si, je…) appear in nearly every sentence.
drills: Learn sam, si, je, smo, ste, su.
Learn the negatives: nisam, nisi, nije…
Make 5 sentences: Ja sam…, Ona je…, Mi smo…

days.title: Week 1 review + numbers 0-20
days.objective: Consolidate week 1 and count to 20.
days.paretoFocus: Weekly consolidation locks in gains; numbers are pure daily utility for prices, time and quantities.

days.title: Present tense verb endings
days.objective: Conjugate regular verbs in the present.
days.paretoFocus: One set of endings (-m, -š, –, -mo, -te, -ju/-e) conjugates most verbs. The ending alone shows who's acting.
drills: Learn raditi: radim, radiš, radi, radimo, radite, rade.

days.title: Core vocabulary: people & family
days.objective: Learn 20 high-frequency family/people words.
days.paretoFocus: Frequency-first vocabulary means every word is one you'll use; family is among the most-discussed topics.
drills: Learn: obitelj, majka, otac, brat, sestra, prijatelj…
Describe your family with imati (to have).

days.title: Nominative case (the subject)
days.objective: Understand the nominative as the dictionary/subject form.
days.paretoFocus: Nominative is the 'home base' of every noun — the form that DOES the action. Knowing what it is makes the other cases make sense.
drills: Note nominative = the form you look up.

items: Point out the nominative in 3 sentences.

days.title: Accusative case (the object)
days.objective: Use the accusative for the direct object.
days.paretoFocus: Nominative + accusative covers the vast majority of basic sentences ('I drink coffee'). This is the highest-leverage grammar in Croatian.

days.title: Negation & yes/no questions
days.objective: Make sentences negative and ask yes/no questions with 'li'.
days.paretoFocus: Negation (ne + verb) and the 'li' question double your range and let you start real exchanges.

days.title: Question words
days.objective: Use tko, što, gdje, kada, zašto, kako, koliko.
days.paretoFocus: Seven question words open every information-seeking sentence.

items: Ask 'Where is the station?' (Gdje je kolodvor?)

days.title: A0 milestone check
days.objective: Prove you can read aloud, greet, introduce yourself and count.
days.paretoFocus: Milestone checks turn scattered knowledge into real capability and reveal exactly what to review.
drills: Take the A0 quiz in the app.
Read a short paragraph aloud cold.

items: Complete the A0 Feynman: 'The Croatian alphabet'.

days.title: imati (to have) & possession
days.objective: Use imati and talk about what you have.
days.paretoFocus: imati is high-frequency and pairs with the accusative you just learned ('Imam auto').
drills: Learn imam, imaš, ima, imamo, imate, imaju.
Say what you have using the accusative.

items: Say 'I have a brother and a sister'.

days.title: More present-tense verbs
days.objective: Expand to 10 high-frequency verbs.
days.paretoFocus: A core set of verbs (ići, htjeti, moći, znati, vidjeti…) covers most of what you'll want to say daily.
drills: Learn ići (to go), znati (to know), vidjeti (to see).

days.title: htjeti & moći (want / can)
days.objective: Express desire and ability with htjeti and moći + infinitive.
days.paretoFocus: These modal-like verbs + an infinitive express wanting and being able — enormous everyday range.

items: Say 'I want to learn' and 'I can speak'.

days.title: Food & ordering
days.objective: Order food and drink and ask the price.
days.paretoFocus: Cafés are where you'll first use Croatian; a few phrases + accusative cover the whole interaction.

days.title: Numbers 20-100 & time
days.objective: Handle prices, phone numbers and telling the time.
days.paretoFocus: Numbers and time are needed for shopping, appointments and travel every single day.
drills: Tell the time: Koliko je sati?

days.title: Adjectives & agreement
days.objective: Describe nouns with adjectives that agree in gender.
days.paretoFocus: Adjective agreement is everywhere; getting the gender pattern early prevents fossilised errors.

items: Say 'a big house' and 'a small city' with agreement.

days.title: Week 3 review + free talk
days.objective: Consolidate verbs and cases in conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Active recall in conversation is where knowledge becomes skill.
drills: 3-minute monologue about yourself and your day.
Re-test biti, imati and core verbs.

days.title: Prepositions & locative (intro)
days.objective: Say where things are with u/na + the locative.
days.paretoFocus: Location is constant in speech; u/na + locative ('u gradu' = in the city) is the most useful preposition pattern.

items: Say 'I am in the city' (U gradu sam).

days.title: Days, months & schedules
days.objective: Talk about days of the week and dates.
days.paretoFocus: Time expressions are pure utility for plans, appointments and small talk.

items: Recite the days of the week.
Say what you do on Monday.

days.title: Daily routine
days.objective: Describe your day in sequence.
days.paretoFocus: Daily routine is one of the most-practised A1 topics and recycles your core verbs.
drills: Sequence with prvo, onda, zatim, na kraju.

items: Tell the time for 3 routine moments.

days.title: Shopping & quantities
days.objective: Buy things and talk about how much/many.
days.paretoFocus: Quantity words and shopping phrases are needed every time you shop or cook.

items: Ask for 'a little bread' and 'a lot of water'.

days.title: Connectors & longer sentences
days.objective: Join ideas with i, ali, jer, pa, onda.
days.paretoFocus: Connectors turn isolated phrases into real discourse — the jump from words to conversation.

days.title: Listening day (comprehensible input)
days.objective: Train your ear with clear Croatian.
days.paretoFocus: Listening is half of communication and the most-neglected skill. Regular input accelerates everything else.
drills: Watch one Easy Croatian video twice.

days.title: The past tense (perfekt) — intro
days.objective: Form the everyday past with biti + past participle.
days.paretoFocus: The perfekt is THE past tense of speech. With it you can tell stories about anything.
drills: Form the past of raditi: radio/radila sam.

items: Say 'I worked' as a man and as a woman.

days.title: Perfekt: gender agreement
days.objective: Make the past participle agree with the subject.
days.paretoFocus: The participle changes with gender/number — a uniquely Slavic feature worth drilling so your past tense sounds right.
drills: Convert 5 sentences to female and plural speakers.

items: Spot a wrong-gender participle and fix it.

days.title: Phase 1 milestone: a real conversation
days.objective: Hold a simple but real conversation about yourself and the past.
days.paretoFocus: Integrating present and past in live speech is the true A1 milestone — proof the core works together.
drills: Cover: who you are, your routine, yesterday.

items: Take the A1 quiz in the app.

days.title: The future tense (futur)
days.objective: Talk about the future with htjeti (short) + infinitive.
days.paretoFocus: The futur is regular and reuses infinitives you know; it completes your past-present-future toolkit.
drills: Learn ću, ćeš, će, ćemo, ćete, će.

days.title: Verb aspect — the big idea
days.objective: Understand imperfective vs perfective verb pairs.
days.paretoFocus: Aspect replaces a lot of English tense machinery. Grasping the concept now saves confusion later.
drills: Decide ongoing vs completed for 5 actions.
Note prefixes often make a verb perfective.

items: Pick the right aspect for 3 examples.

days.title: Genitive case — intro
days.objective: Use the genitive for possession, 'of', and after most numbers.
days.paretoFocus: Genitive is the most-used case after nom/acc — possession, quantities and many prepositions all need it.

days.title: Dative & locative refresher
days.objective: Use the dative (to/for) and revisit the locative (location).
days.paretoFocus: Dative and locative often share endings; learning them together is efficient and covers 'to me' and 'in the city'.
drills: Contrast dative (motion to) vs locative (static in).

items: Say 'I'm giving it to my friend'.

days.title: Describing people & places
days.objective: Use adjectives + cases to describe in detail.
days.paretoFocus: Rich description is a core A1/A2 skill and forces adjective agreement across cases.

days.title: Listening + dictation
days.objective: Sharpen comprehension and spelling through dictation.
days.paretoFocus: Croatian's phonetic spelling makes dictation especially effective — what you hear is exactly what you write.

days.title: Week review + 5-minute monologue
days.objective: Speak for 5 minutes mixing tenses.
days.paretoFocus: Longer monologues build the stamina and automaticity flashcards can't.

days.title: Comparatives
days.objective: Compare things: -iji/-ši and 'nego/od'.
days.paretoFocus: Comparisons appear in opinions and shopping constantly; the core pattern is simple and high-yield.

days.title: Weather & seasons
days.objective: Discuss weather, seasons and clothing.
days.paretoFocus: Weather is a universal small-talk opener and recycles familiar vocabulary and verbs.

days.title: Health & the body
days.objective: Explain how you feel and basic symptoms.
days.paretoFocus: 'Boli me…' is essential survival language for travel and reuses body-part vocabulary.

days.title: Travel & transport
days.objective: Buy tickets and navigate transport.
days.paretoFocus: Transport vocabulary + directions cover the travel experience that motivates many learners.

days.title: Instrumental case — intro
days.objective: Use the instrumental for 'with' and means.
days.paretoFocus: Instrumental covers 'with someone' and 'by (transport)' — common and reuses your travel theme ('autobusom').

days.title: Reading day
days.objective: Read a short text and mine it for language.
days.paretoFocus: Reading builds vocabulary in context and reinforces grammar passively.
drills: Read a short A1/A2 text twice.

days.title: Week review + conversation
days.objective: Use travel, health and weather in a live conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Theme-mixing conversation simulates real life, where topics don't come pre-sorted.

days.title: Expressing opinions
days.objective: Give and justify opinions: mislim da, po meni.
days.paretoFocus: Opinion frames let you say something about anything — the engine of interesting conversation.

days.title: The vocative & addressing people
days.objective: Use the vocative to call or address someone.
days.paretoFocus: The vocative is simple but constant in conversation (Marko → Marko!, gospodine!) and makes you sound natural.

items: Address a friend and a stranger correctly.
Note the vocative is only for addressing.

days.title: Storytelling with aspect
days.objective: Tell a short story using past tense and aspect.
days.paretoFocus: Storytelling integrates tense and aspect — the clearest sign you've internalised the verb system.
drills: Choose imperfective for background, perfective for events.

items: Note one aspect choice to double-check.

days.title: Phone & messages
days.objective: Handle a simple call and write a short message.
days.paretoFocus: Set phrases for calls/messages are formulaic and high-utility.

days.title: Listening sprint
days.objective: Stretch comprehension with faster input.
days.paretoFocus: Gradually increasing audio speed trains real-world listening, the skill learners most often lack.
drills: Watch a video at normal speed.

days.title: Week review + mock interview
days.objective: Answer common questions about yourself fluently.
days.paretoFocus: Rehearsing high-frequency questions builds the automatic responses real conversations demand.
drills: Answer 10 common questions about your life.

days.title: Vocabulary consolidation
days.objective: Lock in your most useful ~600 words.
days.paretoFocus: Reviewing the high-frequency core makes the 20% of words that do 80% of the work automatic.
drills: Do a full Anki review session.
Use 10 review words in fresh sentences.

items: Test yourself on 20 random core words.

days.title: Culture & register (ti/vi)
days.objective: Use the right level of formality in different settings.
days.paretoFocus: Register matters socially; knowing when to use ti vs vi and polite formulas is essential.
drills: Re-do a role-play formally then informally.

days.title: Common mistakes clinic
days.objective: Target the errors you personally keep making (often cases & aspect).
days.paretoFocus: Fixing your top 5 recurring mistakes yields more improvement than new material.

items: Add personal error cards to Anki.

days.title: Free conversation day
days.objective: Have an unscripted 15-minute conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Unscripted speaking is the real test; it builds the spontaneity that defines functional A1+.
drills: Push to use past, present and future.

items: Look up the words you lacked.

days.title: Reading a real text
days.objective: Read an authentic short article, menu or post.
days.paretoFocus: Authentic material proves your skills transfer beyond textbooks and exposes real usage.

days.title: Writing day
days.objective: Write a 150-word text about your life and plans.
days.paretoFocus: Writing forces precision in cases, aspect and spelling and consolidates everything.

days.title: Full cheatsheet review
days.objective: Re-derive the entire one-page summary from memory.
days.paretoFocus: Reproducing the core map proves the 20% is internalised, not just recognised.
drills: Cover the cheatsheet; rebuild each section.
Conjugate biti + one verb in each tense.

items: Check your reconstruction against the app.

days.title: Feynman day: teach it all back
days.objective: Explain Croatian's core systems as if teaching a friend.
days.paretoFocus: Teaching is the strongest test of understanding; gaps surface immediately and get fixed.
drills: Complete all Croatian Feynman concepts in the app.
Re-teach any you can't explain cleanly.

days.title: Mock A1 review
days.objective: Simulate an A1-style test across all four skills.
days.paretoFocus: A timed, four-skill mock reveals exactly where you stand and what to prioritise next.
drills: Do listening, reading, writing and a speaking task.
Take both A0 and A1 quizzes in the app.

days.title: Milestone: solid A1 + your A2 roadmap
days.objective: Confirm A1 mastery and plan the path to A2.
days.paretoFocus: Closing the sprint with a clear next plan turns a 60-day burst into a lasting habit.
drills: Write your A2 plan (genitive/dative/locative mastery, more aspect, vocab).

items: Tick off every A1 can-do statement.
Schedule daily Anki + weekly tutor going forward.
Celebrate — you reached A1 in 60 days!
